subject_tmpl: Question about fence permit at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hi, my name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I own the property at {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. I want to put up a 6-foot wood privacy fence along my back and side yards. Do I need a permit for that, or is there a height limit I should know about? Also wondering if there are rules about how close to the property line the fence can go. Thanks for any guidance.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, yes, fences over 5 feet in height require a zoning permit in Bellwood. The maximum allowed height in a residential rear yard is 6 feet. The fence must be set back at least 1 foot from the property line unless your neighbor provides written consent. Please stop by Village Hall or email us your plat of survey and we can issue the permit same week.

subject_tmpl: Shed permit — do I need one for a 10x12?
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} here, {ADDRESS}. Planning to put in a 10x12 prefab shed in my backyard this summer. My neighbor said anything under 100 square feet doesn't need a permit in Illinois but I want to make sure that's the rule in Bellwood too. Are there setback requirements from the fence or lot line I need to follow? What documents would I need to bring in if a permit is required?
reply_tmpl: Thank you for checking first, {FIRST}. In Bellwood all accessory structures, including sheds, require a zoning review regardless of size. A 10x12 (120 sq ft) does require a permit. Required setbacks are 5 feet from rear and side lot lines. Please bring your plat of survey and a simple site plan showing the proposed shed location. Fee is $50.

subject_tmpl: Deck addition permit process — {STREET}
body_tmpl: Good afternoon. I am looking into adding a wood deck off the back of my house at {ADDRESS}. The deck would be roughly {NUM} square feet and attached to the house. I have a contractor lined up but he needs to know what the Village requires before he submits plans. Is there a permit checklist I can download or should I come in person? What's a realistic timeline for approval?

subject_tmpl: Is my neighbor's fence on my property line?
body_tmpl: Hello — {NAME} at {ADDRESS}. My next-door neighbor recently installed a fence and I believe it may be sitting on my side of the property line. I don't want to start a dispute without checking first. Can the Village send someone out to look, or do I need a licensed surveyor? I have the original plat from when I bought the house in {DATE} if that helps.
reply_tmpl: Hi {NAME}, the Village does not conduct property-line surveys — that would require a licensed land surveyor. However, our code officer can visit to see if the fence violates any Village setback ordinance. If you believe the fence is encroaching on your land, we recommend hiring a surveyor and, if confirmed, filing with the Circuit Court. We're happy to note your concern.

subject_tmpl: Home occupation / home business zoning question
body_tmpl: I'm {FIRST} {LAST} and I live at {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. I'm thinking about running a small bookkeeping business out of my home — no clients coming to the house, just me on the computer. Is that allowed under Bellwood's zoning? Do I need a home occupation permit or a business license, or both? Want to make sure I'm doing this right before I start.
reply_tmpl: Good news, {FIRST} — a no-client-visit home office for a professional service like bookkeeping is generally permitted in residential zones under Bellwood's home occupation ordinance. You will need a Home Occupation Permit ($35) and a Village Business License. Neither sign nor employee visits are allowed. Stop by the Clerk's office with your completed application and a copy of your state license if applicable.

subject_tmpl: Short-term rental — is it allowed in Bellwood?
body_tmpl: Hi there, I've been considering listing my spare bedroom on Airbnb. My address is {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. Before I set anything up I want to know if Bellwood allows short-term rentals in a single-family home. Is there a permit or registration required? Are there any limits on the number of nights or guests? Thank you.

subject_tmpl: Adding a garage — zoning and permit questions for {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: This is {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. My property currently has no garage and I'd like to build a detached two-car garage in the back yard. What are the maximum dimensions allowed? Are there height limits? I'm on a corner lot on {STREET} and {CROSS} so I'm guessing setbacks might be different. Would appreciate any info on the permit process and rough cost.

subject_tmpl: Neighbor running a business out of their home — complaint
body_tmpl: I'm writing to report a concern about the property at {ADDRESS}. There are multiple commercial vehicles parked there daily and people coming and going at all hours. It looks like a contractor operation or something similar. I live on {STREET} and the traffic is affecting our block. Is this type of business allowed in a residential zone? Can code enforcement please check it out? My name is {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for contacting us, {FIRST}. We've logged your concern as case {CASE}. A code officer will conduct a site visit within {DAYS} business days. Commercial vehicle storage and business operations that impact neighbors can violate home occupation rules. We'll keep you posted on the outcome.

subject_tmpl: Zoning question — converting garage to living space
body_tmpl: Hello, I want to convert my attached garage at {ADDRESS} into a family room. I understand I'll need a building permit but I'm not sure if there are any zoning issues with reducing the required off-street parking. My lot is on {STREET} and has a long driveway. Is one driveway space sufficient, or does Bellwood require two off-street spaces? Thank you — {NAME}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {NAME}, Bellwood requires a minimum of two off-street parking spaces for single-family residences. If your driveway provides two spaces without the garage, the conversion is likely approvable with the appropriate building permit. Please submit architectural plans, your plat of survey, and a site plan noting parking. We'll review within 10 business days.

subject_tmpl: Permit needed for roof replacement?
body_tmpl: My roofer says I don't need a permit for a like-for-like shingle replacement on my house at {ADDRESS}. Is that correct for Bellwood? He wants to start on {DATE}. I just want to make sure we're covered and there won't be any issues down the road when I sell. — {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.

subject_tmpl: Abandoned vehicle on private property — {STREET}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} here. There's a car that's been sitting in the driveway at {ADDRESS} on {STREET} for over {DAYS} days with no plates and flat tires. It looks abandoned. Is that a code violation? Can someone come out and address it? The car is an eyesore and takes up space on the street side of the driveway.
reply_tmpl: Thank you {FIRST}. Inoperable or unlicensed vehicles stored outdoors are a code violation. We've created case {CASE} and an officer will inspect within 5 business days. The property owner will be given notice and a compliance deadline. Please call {PHONE} if you have updates.

subject_tmpl: Swimming pool installation — permit and setback requirements
body_tmpl: Good morning. We are planning to install an in-ground swimming pool at our home, {ADDRESS}. We have a contractor ready but need to know Bellwood's setback requirements and what permits are needed. We also want to know if a fence or barrier is required around the pool by Village ordinance. Our lot is roughly {NUM} square feet so space is somewhat limited.

subject_tmpl: Zoning — can I operate a daycare out of my home at {ADDRESS}?
body_tmpl: Hi, I'm {FIRST} {LAST} and I'm looking into becoming a licensed family daycare provider. My home is at {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. Illinois DCFS licensing requires me to confirm my home is in a zone that allows this use. Can you let me know if a family daycare (up to 8 children) is a permitted home occupation in Bellwood? Any permit or special use required?
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, family childcare homes licensed by DCFS are considered a permitted home occupation in Bellwood's residential zones for up to 8 children. You will need a Home Occupation Permit from the Village in addition to your state license. Please contact our office at {PHONE} to start the application. We can usually turn these around within two weeks.

subject_tmpl: Unpermitted addition on neighbor's house — {STREET}
body_tmpl: I'm concerned about recent construction at {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. My neighbor appears to have added a room onto the back of the house. No permit sign was ever posted and the work went pretty fast — finished in about {DAYS} days. I share a property line with this home and want to make sure the work is up to code. Can someone look into this? Name: {FIRST} {LAST}.

subject_tmpl: Zoning verification letter for refinance — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hello, my mortgage lender is asking for a zoning compliance letter or verification that my property at {ADDRESS} is a conforming single-family residential use. Is that something the Village provides? If so, what's the fee and turnaround time? My closing is scheduled for {DATE} so timing is important. Thank you — {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, yes, we issue zoning verification letters. The fee is $75 and standard turnaround is 5 business days. Rush processing (2 business days) is available for $125. Please complete the Zoning Letter Request form on the Village website and submit payment. Email your lender's specific requirements so we can tailor the letter accordingly.

subject_tmpl: Front yard fence — is it allowed?
body_tmpl: Hi — {NAME} at {ADDRESS}. I've seen some houses in Bellwood with low fences in their front yards and I'd like to do the same for safety — I have young kids. Is a front yard fence allowed in Bellwood, and if so what's the maximum height? Does it need to be open style (wrought iron type) or can it be solid? Thanks.
reply_tmpl: Hi {NAME}, front yard fences are allowed in Bellwood up to 3 feet in height. In the front yard, fences must be at least 50% open (picket or wrought-iron style) — solid privacy fences are not permitted in front yards. A zoning permit is required. Please stop by or call us to get started.

subject_tmpl: Solar panel installation permit — what does Bellwood require?
body_tmpl: I'm planning to have solar panels installed on my roof at {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. The solar company mentioned Bellwood may require a permit separate from the electrical permit. Can you confirm what's needed and if there's an HOA or Village design review? I'd also like to know if there are any setback rules for rooftop solar. — {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.

subject_tmpl: Tall weeds / overgrown lot next door — {STREET}
body_tmpl: The vacant lot at {ADDRESS} on {STREET} has not been mowed all season. The weeds are taller than me and I'm worried about rodents. I've called before about this property but nothing seems to have happened. Is there something more I can do? My name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I can be reached at {PHONE}. This is really affecting my property value.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for following up, {FIRST}. We've opened a new case {CASE} for the property at {ADDRESS}. A code officer will inspect and if the overgrowth exceeds the 8-inch ordinance limit, the owner will receive a notice with a {DAYS}-day cure period. If not corrected, the Village can arrange mowing and lien the property. We take repeat complaints seriously.

subject_tmpl: ADU / in-law suite — is it permitted on my lot?
body_tmpl: Good afternoon, I'm {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. I want to add an accessory dwelling unit — basically a small apartment — in my basement for my mother-in-law. Is an ADU or in-law suite allowed in Bellwood? Would it require a separate entrance? Separate utilities? I want to understand the full picture before hiring an architect.

subject_tmpl: Permit status check — {CASE}
body_tmpl: Hi, I submitted a permit application on {DATE} for work at {ADDRESS}. The reference number I was given is {CASE}. I haven't heard back in {DAYS} business days and my contractor is asking for an update. Can you let me know where it stands in the review process? I can be reached at {PHONE}. Thank you — {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, thank you for checking in. Permit {CASE} is currently in plan review. Our building official will complete the review by {DATE}. If corrections are needed, you'll receive an email with the list. We apologize for the delay — we've had higher than normal volume this month. Feel free to call us at Village Hall if you have questions.

subject_tmpl: Junk and debris piled in neighbor's side yard — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: I'm reaching out about a property issue on my block. The house at {ADDRESS} has a side yard full of old furniture, car parts, and miscellaneous junk that's been accumulating for months. It's visible from {STREET} and is becoming a safety and aesthetic issue. I'd like to remain anonymous if possible. Can code enforcement pay a visit?

subject_tmpl: Driveway replacement — do I need a permit?
body_tmpl: {NAME} at {ADDRESS}. I want to replace my concrete driveway with pavers. The contractor said I might need a permit and possibly a public works approval since the apron connects to the Village sidewalk/parkway. Can you clarify the process? The driveway runs along {STREET}. I'd also like to widen it slightly — currently 16 feet, going to 20. Does that change anything?
reply_tmpl: Hi {NAME}, driveway replacement generally requires a permit if the work touches the public right-of-way (the apron). Widening also requires Village approval — maximum driveway width in a residential zone is 20 feet so your plan is at the limit. Please submit a site plan showing the proposed width and material. Public Works will need to sign off on the apron portion.

subject_tmpl: Retaining wall — permit required?
body_tmpl: Hi there. I'm planning to build a retaining wall along the back of my property at {ADDRESS} because of drainage problems. The wall would be about {NUM} feet tall and roughly 30 feet long. My contractor says a permit is required for walls over 4 feet. Is that the rule in Bellwood? And does the engineer need to stamp the plans? — {FIRST} {LAST}.

subject_tmpl: Peeling paint / deteriorating exterior — rental on {STREET}
body_tmpl: I live near a rental property at {ADDRESS} that has severely peeling paint, a sagging gutter, and a broken front step. The property has looked this way for at least {DAYS} days and I don't think anyone is maintaining it. The landlord doesn't live there. Is this something code enforcement handles? My name is {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Thank you {FIRST}. Exterior property maintenance — including paint, gutters, and structural elements like steps — falls under our property maintenance code. We've opened case {CASE} and will inspect within the week. If violations are found the owner of record will receive written notice. We appreciate neighbors who help us keep Bellwood looking its best.

subject_tmpl: Finished basement — permit needed for egress window?
body_tmpl: Hello, I'm finishing my basement at {ADDRESS} and adding a bedroom. My contractor is telling me I need an egress window to meet code. Is that right? Does Bellwood follow the IRC on this? Also, once I add the bedroom do I need to disclose it as a legal bedroom on my property record? Just want everything done correctly. — {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.

subject_tmpl: Parking on front lawn — {STREET} block
body_tmpl: Hi, {NAME} here on the {STREET} block. A household down the street at {ADDRESS} is consistently parking cars on the grass in their front yard, not on the driveway. I know this is against the rules but I'm not sure who to contact. It's tearing up the grass and looks terrible. Can code do something about this? Thank you.
reply_tmpl: Hi {NAME}, parking on an unpaved surface in a front yard is prohibited by Village ordinance. We've noted your report and will dispatch an officer to verify. If a violation is confirmed, the resident will receive a notice of violation and have {DAYS} days to correct the situation. Thank you for letting us know.

subject_tmpl: Satellite dish / antenna rules — residential property
body_tmpl: I'd like to install a large satellite dish on the side of my house at {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. The dish is about 36 inches in diameter. Is a permit required? Are there any height or placement restrictions I should know about? I don't want it on the roof if I can avoid it — I was thinking about mounting it on the chimney or side wall. — {FIRST} {LAST}.

subject_tmpl: Construction without a permit — work on {STREET}
body_tmpl: I want to flag what looks like unpermitted construction happening at {ADDRESS}. Workers have been there since {DATE} putting up what looks like an addition or enclosed porch, but I never saw a permit posted on the property as required. I'm {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS} nearby. Would appreciate a code enforcement check.
reply_tmpl: Thank you, {FIRST}. We've opened case {CASE} for the address. Our building official will verify whether a permit was pulled and if not, will issue a stop-work order pending permit application. Unpermitted construction is taken seriously and the property owner may face fees. We'll follow up with you.

subject_tmpl: Zoning — can I split my large lot into two parcels?
body_tmpl: Hello, my name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I own the double lot at {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. I've been thinking about selling the back portion as a buildable lot. Is a lot split possible in Bellwood and what's the process? Are there minimum lot size requirements I need to worry about? I know it involves Cook County too — just trying to understand the Village side first.

subject_tmpl: What is my property's zoning classification?
body_tmpl: Hi — I'm doing some planning for my property at {ADDRESS} and realized I don't actually know what zoning district it's in. I've been here {AGE} years and it never came up. Can you look it up and let me know? Also curious what uses are allowed as-of-right and whether I can build a second story addition. — {NAME}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {NAME}, your property at {ADDRESS} is zoned R-2, Single-Family Residential. Permitted as-of-right uses include single-family dwellings, accessory structures, and certain home occupations. A second-story addition is generally allowed subject to height limits (35 ft max) and setback compliance. Please contact us for a full list of permitted uses or to schedule a pre-application meeting.

subject_tmpl: Short-term rental concerns — noise and parking on {STREET}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} here. I believe the house at {ADDRESS} on {STREET} is being rented out on Airbnb. There are new people there almost every weekend, noise at all hours, and parking is a constant problem. Does Bellwood regulate short-term rentals? If the property isn't licensed, is that a code violation? I'd like something done before this summer gets worse.

subject_tmpl: Portable storage container — how long can it stay?
body_tmpl: We're doing a major renovation at {ADDRESS} and the contractor wants to park a PODS container in our driveway or on the street for up to {DAYS} days. Is that allowed in Bellwood? Is there a permit or fee? I just want to be a good neighbor and make sure we're following the rules. Thank you — {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, portable storage containers require a temporary placement permit from the Village. On private property (driveway) they are allowed up to 30 days with a permit ($25). Placement on a public street requires additional Public Works approval and is generally limited to 7 days. Please apply at Village Hall before the container is delivered.

subject_tmpl: Fence in disrepair — falling into my yard
body_tmpl: Good morning. My neighbor's fence at {ADDRESS} on {STREET} is falling apart — several panels have collapsed and are now leaning into my yard. The neighbor hasn't responded to my texts. I'm worried it's going to damage my garden or be a hazard for my kids. Is this something the Village can require them to fix? Name: {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, a fence in disrepair that poses a hazard is a code violation under our property maintenance ordinance. Please email us a photo and the exact address and we'll open a case. The owner will be notified and given {DAYS} days to repair or remove the fence. Thank you for reaching out.

subject_tmpl: Generator installation — permit requirements at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hello, I want to install a standby natural gas generator at my home at {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. My electrician said I'll need both an electrical and a mechanical permit from the Village. Is that right? Are there setback requirements from the house or lot line? Any noise ordinance restrictions I should know about? — {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.

subject_tmpl: RV parked on street for weeks — {CROSS} and {STREET}
body_tmpl: There's a large RV that has been parked on {STREET} near {CROSS} for about {DAYS} days without moving. It's blocking sight lines at the intersection and taking up two spots. Is there a limit on how long a vehicle can be parked on a Village street? Can code or police address this? — {NAME}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {NAME}, Bellwood ordinance limits street parking for oversized vehicles like RVs to 48 hours. We've forwarded your complaint to our police liaison as well as code enforcement. Case {CASE} has been opened. Officers will follow up within 24 hours. Thank you.

subject_tmpl: Contractor left without a final inspection — what do I do?
body_tmpl: My contractor finished a bathroom addition at {ADDRESS} back in {DATE} but never scheduled the final inspection. Now they're not returning my calls. I have a permit on file — reference {CASE} — but it shows open. Can I request the final inspection myself, or does it have to be the contractor? I want to close this out properly. — {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, yes, as the property owner you can request the final inspection directly. Please call our office or use the online portal referencing permit {CASE} to schedule. Bring any documentation the contractor left about the work completed. If the inspection passes, we'll close the permit. If there are deficiencies, we'll provide a written punch list.

subject_tmpl: Zoning — home salon / barbershop from residence
body_tmpl: Hi there. I'm a licensed cosmetologist and I'm wondering if I can run a small one-chair hair salon out of my home at {ADDRESS}. I'd have clients come by appointment only — maybe 3-4 per day. Is that allowed in Bellwood? I know some municipalities require a separate entrance for client-facing home businesses. What would I need to do? — {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.

subject_tmpl: Bright exterior security light shining into my bedroom — {STREET}
body_tmpl: The property at {ADDRESS} installed a very bright LED security light that shines directly into my bedroom window. I haven't been able to sleep properly since {DATE}. Is there a light trespass or nuisance lighting ordinance in Bellwood? I've spoken to the neighbor but they won't adjust it. I'd appreciate the Village's help. — {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Good morning {FIRST}. Nuisance lighting that unreasonably disturbs neighboring properties can fall under Bellwood's general nuisance ordinance. We'll have a code officer visit at dusk to assess the situation. If the light is determined to be a violation, the property owner will be asked to redirect or shield it within {DAYS} days. We'll be in touch.

subject_tmpl: Hardscape / patio permit question for {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hello — {NAME} at {ADDRESS}. I'd like to add a patio in my backyard using concrete pavers, roughly {NUM} square feet. My yard drains to the rear. Do I need a permit for this, and are there any impervious surface limits I should know about? I don't want to create drainage problems for my neighbors on {CROSS}.

subject_tmpl: Tree removal on property line — who's responsible?
body_tmpl: There's a large tree on or near the property line between my house at {ADDRESS} and my neighbor at {ADDRESS}. The tree looks diseased and some large branches are hanging over my garage. My neighbor says it's my tree; I think it's theirs. Can the Village help determine who is responsible? What happens if neither of us acts and it falls?
reply_tmpl: Hi, property line tree disputes are ultimately a civil matter between landowners, but our arborist can advise if the tree poses a public safety hazard. If the tree is on public right-of-way, the Village will handle it. Please provide the exact address and we can arrange a staff review. In the meantime, we recommend consulting a certified arborist and documenting the condition.

subject_tmpl: HVAC replacement — permit needed?
body_tmpl: My furnace and central AC are being replaced next week at {ADDRESS}. The HVAC company says they'll pull the permits, but I want to confirm what's required from the Village side. Should I ask them for permit numbers? And will there be an inspection of the equipment after installation? Just want to make sure everything is documented for insurance purposes. — {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, yes, HVAC replacement requires a mechanical permit from Bellwood. A reputable contractor will pull this automatically. You can verify the permit was issued by calling Village Hall with your address or checking our online permit portal. An inspection is required before equipment is closed in or drywall replaced. Ask your contractor to schedule it.

subject_tmpl: Kennel / multiple dogs at neighbor's house — noise and odor
body_tmpl: I'm writing about the property at {ADDRESS}. My neighbor appears to be breeding or keeping a large number of dogs — I can hear constant barking at all hours and the smell is very noticeable in warm weather. I believe there are more than the allowed number of pets. Can code enforcement or animal control look into this? — {FIRST} {LAST}, {STREET}.

subject_tmpl: Variance request — building too close to side lot line
body_tmpl: Dear Building Office — I am hoping to add a small mudroom to the north side of my house at {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. However, my lot is only {NUM} feet wide and there would be less than 4 feet between the new addition and the property line. I understand this may require a variance. How do I apply for one and what's the process at the Zoning Board of Appeals? — {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Thank you {FIRST}. A side yard variance would need to go before Bellwood's Zoning Board of Appeals. Applications are due by the {DATE} of each month for the following month's hearing. The fee is $200 and requires a completed application, plat of survey, site plan, and neighbor notification. We recommend scheduling a pre-application meeting with our office first.

subject_tmpl: Building permit fees — can you send me the schedule?
body_tmpl: Hi, I'm planning several projects at my property at {ADDRESS} over the next year — a fence, a deck, and possibly a room addition. Before I budget everything out I'd love to get a copy of the current Village building permit fee schedule. Is that available on the website or can someone email it to me? — {NAME}, {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {NAME}, I've attached our current permit fee schedule. Highlights: fence permits $50, deck permits calculated at $8 per $1,000 of construction value (min. $75), and addition permits also at $8 per $1,000 (min. $150). Zoning review is included. Let us know when you're ready to apply and we can walk you through the process.

subject_tmpl: I received a code violation notice — can I appeal?
body_tmpl: I received a notice of violation dated {DATE} regarding my property at {ADDRESS}, case number {CASE}. I disagree with the citation — the item cited (a shed) has been there for over {AGE} years and was there when I bought the house. How do I appeal this? Is there a hearing process? I want to handle this properly. — {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, you have the right to appeal within 15 days of receiving the notice. Please submit a written appeal request to the Village Clerk's office referencing case {CASE}. A hearing will be scheduled before the Administrative Hearing Officer and you'll be notified of the date. We'd also recommend bringing any documentation showing the shed's existence prior to your ownership.
